pattern: The value at an index only counts toward the sum when the index's own binary representation carries the target number of ones, so the whole task reduces to a popcount filter over positions rather than over the values themselves.
hints: It's the index's own binary form that matters here, not the value stored at that index.
Count the 1 bits of each index i from 0 up to the array's last position.
Add nums[i] into a running total whenever that popcount equals k, and return the total once you've scanned every index.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, five values and k = 1
statement example, four values and k = 2
a single-element array where the only index has zero set bits and k = 0
a single-element array where the only index has zero set bits but k = 1, so nothing matches
k = 0 only ever matches index 0, no matter how many other indices exist
several indices in the same array share exactly one set bit, so all of them must be accumulated
the maximum k with a maximum-length array, which can never actually be satisfied since no index below the length limit has that many set bits

stress.description: random arrays across the full length and value range with a random k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, 10)
    return [nums, k]

stress.description: smaller random arrays paired with every possible k value in rotation
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = n % 11
    return [nums, k]
